content.thinking:
83% showdown win rate is huge - villain wins showdowns a lot. With 3s8c from the SB (seat 0), I'm in position as dealer/SB. To call is 1 chip. With 3s8c, this is a weak hand. The opponent has PFR of 10%, low aggression. 

With 3s8c I should just fold - it's a weak hand and villain wins showdowns at 84%. The call is only 1 chip though into a 3-chip pot... that's good pot odds. But 3-8 offsuit is still quite weak.


The pot odds are tempting me to call, but villain's 84% showdown win rate and low fold-to-cbet of 29% means I can't generate enough fold equity to make this profitable - I'm better off folding this weak hand.

content.text:
**Analysis:**
- **Hand strength:** 3s-8o is one of the weakest preflop hands — very poor equity even if I hit a piece of the board.
- **Opponent profile:** VPIP 64%, meaning villain plays a very wide range, but critically wins **31/37 showdowns (84%)** — a sign villain reaches showdown with strong holdings and doesn't bluff off chips.
- **Fold-to-cbet is only 29%** — bluffing postflop won't be effective, and I can't rep much with 3-8o.
- **Low PFR (10%)** means villain won't be bluffing me off pots with big aggression either, making it hard to fold them out.

Calling 1 into a 3-chip pot offers decent immediate odds, but 3-8o plays terribly multi-street against a loose-passive opponent who rarely folds and wins showdowns at an elite rate. Best to fold and wait for a better spot.

- Villain is very loose preflop (VPIP 79%) — raising with Q4h for value is poor since they'll call wide

content.text:
**Analysis:**
- Villain is extremely loose (VPIP 91%) and passive (PFR 25%) — limp-called here
- Folds to flop c-bets only 39% — hard to push off hands with a c-bet alone
- Folds to **river** bets 69% (11/16) — the profitable street to apply pressure
- Showdown win rate 88% — when villain calls to showdown, they have it; avoid thin value at showdown
- We hold 7s6c — a speculative hand, out of position postflop as BB

**Decision:** 7s6c has decent playability (straight/two-pair potential) but is a marginal raise candidate OOP against a sticky caller. Checking to see a free flop, then c-betting selectively and applying river pressure is the higher-EV line given villain's tendencies.

content.text:

- Villain VPIP 95% / PFR 28% — extremely loose-passive, calls almost everything preflop
- Folds to flop c-bets only 39% (11/28) — sticky on the flop

content.text:

- Villain is loose-passive (VPIP 61%, PFR 17%) — limped in, consistent with pattern

- Villain stack is short (158 chips)
